original_hypothesis.title: BondingTax.updateSwapParams lacks slippage bounds, allowing slippage > 100% and minOutput underflow-style behavior (minOutput=0) harming treasury swaps
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: BondingTax.updateSwapParams sets `_slippage` without bounding it to <= 10000. In swapForAsset, minOutput = expectedOutput * (10000 - _slippage) / 10000. If _slippage > 10000, (10000 - _slippage) underflows and reverts in Solidity 0.8, causing swapForAsset to revert permanently; if _slippage is set to exactly 10000, minOutput becomes 0, removing slippage protection and allowing extremely bad execution prices.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a configuration-sensitive but code-level missing validation on a safety-critical parameter. Even with a trusted admin, mistakes happen; if admin key compromised (out of scope for HIGH realism), attacker can set catastrophic values. Validate whether the system has guardrails elsewhere.
deployment_assumptions: ADMIN_ROLE holder sets _slippage to >= 10000 (or >10000) via updateSwapParams
swapForAsset is relied upon for ongoing tax conversion

proof_of_signal.pattern: _slippage assigned directly from input; later used in (10000 - _slippage) without bounds check

preconditions: swapForAsset is invoked by bondingRouter after taxes accumulate

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: slippage=10000 makes minOutput=0; slippage>10000 reverts swapForAsset

analysis_questions: Is there an operational policy ensuring slippage bounds, or any timelock/DAO gating for this admin action?
Can swapForAsset failure halt other critical protocol operations (e.g., trading via router calling swapForAsset)?
validation_requirements: Set _slippage=10000 and observe minOutput=0 execution behavior
Set _slippage>10000 and confirm swapForAsset reverts, causing cascading failures

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If misconfigured, tax swaps execute with no slippage floor (or revert forever), harming treasury conversion and potentially breaking buy/sell flows if they synchronously call swapForAsset.
prerequisites: TaxManager configured such that swaps are triggered in-line with trades

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Medium: treasury receives worse swap rates or swaps halt, accumulating taxToken exposure
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Protocol treasury and any users impacted by trade paths that trigger swaps
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: High if admin can correct config; low if privileges lost

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing bounds tests for slippage and swapForAsset revert handling

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: On-chain: SwapFailed events spike or swapForAsset reverts

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
